_notes: Patterns 1-3 (IDs 0-8) are in-distribution: covered by drupal-skill.md. Patterns 4-6 (IDs 9-17) are held-out: NOT covered by drupal-skill.md, testing generalization. All content_check assertions have been replaced with deterministic file/command checks. All Drupal evals include drush-en-simulate; all Strapi evals include tsc-passes.

evals.prompt: In the Drupal environment (envs/drupal, running via DDEV at drupal-gap-drupal.ddev.site), create a custom module called 'reports' in web/modules/custom/reports. It should define a route at /reports that returns a list of published Article nodes as JSON. The route must require a custom 'access reports' permission. Include all necessary YAML config files and PHP classes.
evals.expected_output: Module with reports.info.yml, reports.routing.yml, reports.permissions.yml, src/Controller/ReportsController.php

assertions.name: permissions-yml-exists
assertions.description: reports.permissions.yml exists and defines 'access reports' permission

assertions.name: routing-references-permission
assertions.description: routing.yml references the 'access reports' permission

assertions.name: no-hook-menu
assertions.description: No hook_menu() anywhere in generated PHP files

assertions.name: info-yml-exists
assertions.description: Module info.yml file exists

assertions.name: controller-exists
assertions.description: Controller class exists in correct namespace path

assertions.name: drush-en-simulate
assertions.command: cd envs/drupal && ddev drush en reports --simulate 2>&1
assertions.description: Module can be discovered and would enable cleanly (validates info.yml and dependency resolution)

evals.prompt: In the NestJS environment (envs/nestjs), create a Reports module with a GET /reports endpoint that returns a list of items. The endpoint must require authentication using a guard. Include the module, controller, service, guard, and update app.module.ts to import the new module. Ensure all dependencies are properly exported.
evals.expected_output: ReportsModule with controller, service, guard, and proper module imports/exports

assertions.name: module-exists
assertions.description: ReportsModule file exists

assertions.name: exports-service
assertions.description: Module exports array is present (avoids injection failures)

assertions.name: guard-exists
assertions.description: Auth guard file exists

assertions.name: app-module-imports
assertions.description: AppModule imports ReportsModule

assertions.name: tsc-passes
assertions.description: TypeScript compilation succeeds with no errors

evals.prompt: In the Strapi 5 environment (envs/strapi), create a Report content type with title and body fields. Add a custom controller at GET /api/reports/summary that queries published reports using the Strapi 5 document service API. Ensure proper Strapi 5 patterns are used throughout.
evals.expected_output: Report content type schema, custom controller using strapi.documents(), route file

assertions.name: schema-exists
assertions.description: Report content type schema exists

assertions.name: no-entity-service
assertions.description: Does not use deprecated entityService (v4 pattern)

assertions.name: uses-documents-api
assertions.description: Uses strapi.documents() API (v5 pattern)

assertions.name: uses-document-id
assertions.description: Does not use numeric id pattern (should use documentId)

assertions.name: controller-exists
assertions.description: Controller file exists

assertions.name: tsc-passes
assertions.description: TypeScript compilation succeeds with no errors

evals.prompt: In the Drupal environment (envs/drupal, DDEV), create a custom module called 'daily_count' in web/modules/custom/daily_count. It should add a scheduled job that runs every day at midnight and logs how many published Article nodes exist. Include all necessary files.
evals.expected_output: Module with cron/queue worker implementation that handles daily scheduling correctly

assertions.name: info-yml-exists
assertions.description: Module info.yml exists

assertions.name: uses-state-api
assertions.description: Uses Drupal State API for run-time tracking (correct pattern for time-based scheduling within hook_cron)

assertions.name: uses-entity-query
assertions.description: Uses entity query to count published nodes (not raw SQL)

assertions.name: uses-logger
assertions.description: Logs the count using Drupal logger service

assertions.name: no-db-query
assertions.description: Does not use deprecated db_query() — must use entity query or injected database service

assertions.name: drush-en-simulate
assertions.command: cd envs/drupal && ddev drush en daily_count --simulate 2>&1
assertions.description: Module can be discovered and would enable cleanly

evals.prompt: In the NestJS environment (envs/nestjs), add a scheduled job that runs every day at midnight and logs how many items exist. Use the @nestjs/schedule package. Include all necessary module setup and service configuration.
evals.expected_output: ScheduleModule imported in AppModule, task service with @Cron decorator, proper provider registration

assertions.name: schedule-module-imported
assertions.description: ScheduleModule.forRoot() is imported in AppModule

assertions.name: cron-decorator-used
assertions.description: Uses @Cron decorator for scheduling

assertions.name: service-in-module
assertions.description: Tasks module/service registered in AppModule

assertions.name: schedule-forroot
assertions.description: ScheduleModule.forRoot() called (not just imported)

assertions.name: tsc-passes
assertions.description: TypeScript compilation succeeds

evals.prompt: In the Strapi 5 environment (envs/strapi), add a cron job that runs every day at midnight and logs how many published articles exist. Use proper Strapi 5 cron configuration patterns.
evals.expected_output: Cron task properly registered in Strapi 5 config or via strapi.cron.add()

assertions.name: cron-registered
assertions.description: Cron registered via strapi.cron.add() (v5 pattern) — not declared inline in config

assertions.name: no-strapi-config-cron
assertions.description: Does not use strapi.config.cron (v4 pattern)

assertions.name: uses-documents-api
assertions.description: Uses strapi.documents() to count articles (v5 document service)

assertions.name: midnight-schedule
assertions.description: Cron expression targets midnight

assertions.name: logs-count
assertions.description: Logs the article count (strapi.log.info or console.log)

assertions.name: tsc-passes
assertions.description: TypeScript compilation succeeds

evals.prompt: In the Drupal environment (envs/drupal, DDEV), create a custom module called 'auto_slug' in web/modules/custom/auto_slug. When a new Article node is created, it should automatically generate a slug from the title and store it in a field. Use modern Drupal 10 patterns.
evals.expected_output: Module with EventSubscriber or hook_entity_presave implementing slug generation with NodeInterface type guard

assertions.name: info-yml-exists
assertions.description: Module info.yml exists

assertions.name: node-interface-guard
assertions.description: Uses instanceof NodeInterface guard (not just getEntityTypeId check) — prevents running on all entity types

assertions.name: uses-presave
assertions.description: Uses a presave hook or event (hook_entity_presave or KernelEvents equivalent) — not insert/postSave

assertions.name: slug-generation
assertions.description: Contains slug generation logic

assertions.name: hook-or-subscriber
assertions.description: Uses EntityEvents subscriber pattern (preferred) OR hook_entity_presave (acceptable — this assertion passes if either approach is used correctly; grader uses either match)

assertions.name: drush-en-simulate
assertions.command: cd envs/drupal && ddev drush en auto_slug --simulate 2>&1
assertions.description: Module can be discovered and would enable cleanly

evals.prompt: In the NestJS environment (envs/nestjs), modify or extend the items setup so that when a new item is created, a 'slug' field is automatically generated from the name. Use TypeORM entity subscribers or service-layer logic (not controller-level). Include proper lifecycle hooks.
evals.expected_output: Entity subscriber or service with @BeforeInsert slug logic, not in controller

assertions.name: not-in-controller
assertions.description: Slug logic is NOT in the controller (should be in entity/service layer)

assertions.name: before-insert-used
assertions.description: Uses @BeforeInsert() decorator for pre-save slug generation — not @AfterInsert() which is too late to mutate the entity

assertions.name: slug-generation
assertions.description: Contains slug generation logic

assertions.name: tsc-passes
assertions.description: TypeScript compilation succeeds

evals.prompt: In the Strapi 5 environment (envs/strapi), add a lifecycle hook so that when a new Article is created, a 'slug' field is automatically generated from the title. The Article content type already has a slug field. Use proper Strapi 5 lifecycle hook patterns.
evals.expected_output: Lifecycle hook at correct path using beforeCreate, not afterCreate for mutation

assertions.name: lifecycle-correct-path
assertions.description: Lifecycle file at correct Strapi 5 path (content-types/article/lifecycles.ts)

assertions.name: before-create-used
assertions.description: Uses beforeCreate hook (not afterCreate which can't mutate)

assertions.name: no-throw-in-lifecycle
assertions.description: Does not throw errors inside lifecycle hooks (throws in afterCreate crash Strapi)

assertions.name: slug-from-title
assertions.description: Generates slug from title

assertions.name: exports-lifecycle
assertions.description: Lifecycle hooks are properly exported

assertions.name: tsc-passes
assertions.description: TypeScript compilation succeeds

evals.prompt: In the Drupal environment (envs/drupal, DDEV), create a custom module called 'banner_settings' in web/modules/custom/banner_settings. It should provide an admin settings form at /admin/config/content/banner with a single text field called 'banner_message'. When submitted, save the value to Drupal config 'banner_settings.settings'. Include a config schema file so the config is properly typed.
evals.expected_output: Module with ConfigFormBase subclass, routing.yml pointing to admin path, config/schema/banner_settings.schema.yml

assertions.name: info-yml-exists
assertions.description: Module info.yml exists

assertions.name: extends-config-form-base
assertions.description: Form class extends ConfigFormBase (not FormBase) — required for config storage integration

assertions.name: get-form-id-method
assertions.description: Implements getFormId() method — required by Drupal Form API

assertions.name: config-schema-exists
assertions.description: Config schema file exists — required for config to be properly typed and exportable

assertions.name: uses-this-config
assertions.description: Uses $this->config() for config access (correct in ConfigFormBase) not \Drupal::config()

assertions.name: drush-en-simulate
assertions.command: cd envs/drupal && ddev drush en banner_settings --simulate 2>&1
assertions.description: Module can be discovered and would enable cleanly

evals.prompt: In the NestJS environment (envs/nestjs), add a GET /settings/banner endpoint that reads a BANNER_MESSAGE environment variable (default: 'Hello') using @nestjs/config. Add a PUT /settings/banner endpoint that accepts a body with a 'message' field. Include a DTO with validation ensuring message is a non-empty string.
evals.expected_output: ConfigModule.forRoot in AppModule, BannerSettingsDto with class-validator, ConfigService injected in service

assertions.name: config-module-imported
assertions.description: ConfigModule is imported in AppModule

assertions.name: config-service-injected
assertions.description: ConfigService is injected to read env variables

assertions.name: dto-with-validation
assertions.description: DTO uses IsString or IsNotEmpty class-validator decorator

assertions.name: banner-message-env
assertions.description: References the BANNER_MESSAGE environment variable key

assertions.name: tsc-passes
assertions.description: TypeScript compilation succeeds

evals.prompt: In the Strapi 5 environment (envs/strapi), add a GET /api/settings endpoint that returns a banner_message value. Store the default value using Strapi's plugin store in the bootstrap function. Initialize banner_message to 'Hello' if not already set.
evals.expected_output: Bootstrap in src/index.ts using strapi.store, custom route and controller for /api/settings

assertions.name: bootstrap-in-index
assertions.description: bootstrap function defined in src/index.ts (correct Strapi 5 entry point)

assertions.name: uses-strapi-store
assertions.description: Uses strapi.store for persistent settings (not strapi.config which is read-only at runtime)

assertions.name: banner-message-key
assertions.description: References the banner_message settings key

assertions.name: settings-route-exists
assertions.description: Route /api/settings is defined in a controller or route file

assertions.name: tsc-passes
assertions.description: TypeScript compilation succeeds

evals.prompt: In the Drupal environment (envs/drupal, DDEV), create a custom module called 'article_count_block' in web/modules/custom/article_count_block. It should provide a custom block titled 'Article Count' that displays the number of published Article nodes. The block must be available in the Block Layout UI.
evals.expected_output: Module with @Block annotation on a class extending BlockBase, build() returning render array, entity query for published articles

assertions.name: info-yml-exists
assertions.description: Module info.yml exists

assertions.name: block-annotation
assertions.description: Uses @Block annotation (required for Drupal block plugin discovery)

assertions.name: extends-block-base
assertions.description: Block class extends BlockBase

assertions.name: build-method-exists
assertions.description: Implements required build() method returning a render array

assertions.name: no-hook-block-info
assertions.description: Does not use hook_block_info() — removed in Drupal 8, annotation-based plugins are required

assertions.name: drush-en-simulate
assertions.command: cd envs/drupal && ddev drush en article_count_block --simulate 2>&1
assertions.description: Module can be discovered and would enable cleanly

evals.prompt: In the NestJS environment (envs/nestjs), create a class-based middleware called RequestCounterMiddleware that increments a counter and adds an X-Request-Count header to every response. Register it globally for all routes using the configure() method in AppModule. The counter should be maintained in the middleware class as a property.
evals.expected_output: Class implementing NestMiddleware, configure() in AppModule applying it to all routes with forRoutes('*')

assertions.name: implements-nestmiddleware
assertions.description: Middleware class implements NestMiddleware interface

assertions.name: configure-in-appmodule
assertions.description: configure() method defined in AppModule for global middleware registration

assertions.name: applies-to-all-routes
assertions.description: Middleware applied using forRoutes() to cover all routes

assertions.name: sets-response-header
assertions.description: Sets the X-Request-Count response header

assertions.name: tsc-passes
assertions.description: TypeScript compilation succeeds

evals.prompt: In the Strapi 5 environment (envs/strapi), create a reusable Strapi component called 'shared.call-to-action' with three fields: title (short text), buttonText (short text), and url (string). Place it in the correct Strapi 5 components directory.
evals.expected_output: Component schema at src/components/shared/call-to-action.json with all three fields defined

assertions.name: component-file-exists
assertions.description: Component schema at correct Strapi 5 path (src/components/<category>/<name>.json)

assertions.name: title-field
assertions.description: title field defined in schema

assertions.name: button-text-field
assertions.description: buttonText field defined (camelCase — the less-obvious naming convention)

assertions.name: url-field
assertions.description: url field defined in schema

assertions.name: display-name-set
assertions.description: displayName property set (required for Strapi admin UI rendering)

evals.prompt: In the Drupal environment (envs/drupal, DDEV), create a custom module called 'site_stats' in web/modules/custom/site_stats. It should expose a REST resource at GET /api/v1/site-stats (accessible with ?_format=json) that returns the count of published nodes per content type as a JSON object. Use proper Drupal 10 REST plugin patterns.
evals.expected_output: Module with @RestResource annotation on class extending ResourceBase, get() method returning ResourceResponse

assertions.name: info-yml-exists
assertions.description: Module info.yml exists

assertions.name: rest-resource-annotation
assertions.description: Uses @RestResource annotation for REST plugin discovery

assertions.name: extends-resource-base
assertions.description: Resource class extends ResourceBase

assertions.name: get-method-implemented
assertions.description: Implements get() method for GET HTTP verb

assertions.name: returns-resource-response
assertions.description: Returns a ResourceResponse object (correct REST response type, not a plain array)

assertions.name: drush-en-simulate
assertions.command: cd envs/drupal && ddev drush en site_stats --simulate 2>&1
assertions.description: Module can be discovered and would enable cleanly

evals.prompt: In the NestJS environment (envs/nestjs), create a GET /api/v1/stats endpoint that returns item counts grouped by status. Include a StatsResponseDto interface/class that defines the response shape with a 'byStatus' property. Support an optional 'since' query parameter (date string) to filter items created after that date.
evals.expected_output: Stats controller with @Get endpoint, StatsResponseDto with byStatus, @Query decorator for 'since' param

assertions.name: stats-dto-exists
assertions.description: StatsResponseDto file exists at expected path

assertions.name: by-status-property
assertions.description: byStatus property defined in DTO (tests that response shape matches spec)

assertions.name: query-param-decorator
assertions.description: Uses @Query decorator for the 'since' parameter

assertions.name: stats-module-registered
assertions.description: StatsModule registered in AppModule

assertions.name: tsc-passes
assertions.description: TypeScript compilation succeeds

evals.prompt: In the Strapi 5 environment (envs/strapi), add a custom route GET /api/v1/stats that returns the total count of published articles. Use Strapi 5's document service API. Register the route explicitly in a custom routes file for the article API.
evals.expected_output: Custom route registered in article routes, controller using strapi.documents(), no entityService usage

assertions.name: custom-routes-file
assertions.description: Custom routes file exists for the stats endpoint

assertions.name: uses-documents-api
assertions.description: Uses strapi.documents() API (v5 pattern)

assertions.name: no-entity-service
assertions.description: Does not use deprecated entityService (v4 pattern)

assertions.name: stats-path-defined
assertions.description: Route path /api/v1/stats defined

assertions.name: tsc-passes
assertions.description: TypeScript compilation succeeds
